title: Ancient bones show dogs have been woven into human life for nearly 16,000 years
description: Based on international collaborations lasting decades, 2 new studies have unlocked previously unavailable information from the bones of dogs long dead.

title: Ancient Statue Reveals Shocking Secrets Through CT Scan
description: A recent scientific investigation has unveiled astonishing secrets hidden within a 1500-year-old Buddhist statue. Discovered by Dutch researchers, the statue contains a mummy in a meditative position, challenging our understanding of ancient practices. This revelation has sparked widespread interest and discussion, as it highlights the mysteries still buried beneath our feet. The findings raise questions about the rituals of Buddhist monks and their unique practices. As science continues to explore the past, more incredible discoveries await. Dive into this fascinating story to learn more about the secrets of this ancient artifact.

title: EXCLUSIVE: Brandon Lee Was Terrified of 'Ancient' Family Death 'Curse' Before He Was Killed in Tragic on-Set Accident 33 Years Ago — 'He Said He Would Never Live to See 30'
description: Brandon Lee died in a tragic on-set accident while filming 'The Crow' in 1993.

title: Iraq’s ancient ruins must wait a bit longer to be uncovered
description: article author: AFP Author: Wed, 2026-04-01 02:30 BAGHDAD: When you’re in a hole, it’s often said, stop digging. Unfortunately for international archeologists in Iraq, the Middle East war has forced them to do just that. There are ruins in Iraq from some of the world’s earliest civilizations dating back thousands of years, and up to 60 teams would normally have been working on digs. But “all of these missions have left,” an government official in Baghdad said. Adelheid Otto, 59, from the University of Munich, began a dig at ancient Shuruppak, modern-day Tell Fara, on Feb. 28 — the day the war began. Main category: Middle-East Tags: Iraq Iraq’s ancient ruins
